name: Profile_R5_Contract_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.Contract resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 Contract resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Contract resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.Contract resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 Contract resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 Contract resources.

element.short: R5: Why is action (not) needed? additional types
element.comment:
Element `Contract.term.action.reason` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.term.action.reasonCode` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
Element `Contract.term.action.reason` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.term.action.reasonReference` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
Element `Contract.term.action.reason` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.term.action.reason` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Contract.term.action.reason` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.

Element `Contract.friendly.content[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.friendly.content[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Contract.friendly.content[x]` with unmapped reference targets: DocumentReference.
Source element `Contract.friendly.content[x]` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `Contract.friendly.content[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.

Element `Contract.legal.content[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.legal.content[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Contract.legal.content[x]` with unmapped reference targets: DocumentReference.
Source element `Contract.legal.content[x]` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `Contract.legal.content[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.

element.definition: R5: `Contract.rule.content[x]` additional types from child elements (display, identifier, reference, type)
element.comment:
Element `Contract.rule.content[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.rule.content[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Contract.rule.content[x]` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: display, identifier, reference, type.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Contract.rule.content[x]` with unmapped reference targets: DocumentReference.
Source element `Contract.rule.content[x]` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `Contract.rule.content[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.

Element `Contract.legallyBinding[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Contract.legallyBinding[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Contract.legallyBinding[x]` with unmapped reference targets: DocumentReference.
Source element `Contract.legallyBinding[x]` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `Contract.legallyBinding[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.
